title: Boat Rental in Saint Tropez: Costs, Seasons, and Tips for 2026
html:
Commanding the French Riviera, Saint Tropez offers a glamorous setting for boating, with departures from Port de Saint Tropez and prices starting from around €400 for a half-day motorboat rental. To explore the coast, you can compare verified listings on BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace for boat rentals.

Where to set off in Saint Tropez?

Head to the old port of Saint Tropez, Port de Saint Tropez, for the widest choice of rental bases. Other departure points include Port Grimaud, a charming marina town to the south, and the anchorage at Pampelonne Bay, famous for its sandy beaches. For longer trips, sail from Port Cogolin or anchor off Île de Porquerolles, a stunning island in the Hyères archipelago, some 20 nautical miles south-west. You can hire a boat in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Motorboat, ideal for zipping between coves, up to 8 guests, from €400 half-day.
  2. Catamaran, stable and spacious for day trips, up to 12 guests, from €1,200 full-day.
  3. Crewed yacht, luxury with skipper and host, up to 12 guests, from €3,000 full-day.
  4. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), fast and nimble for water sports, up to 6 guests, from €350 half-day.
  5. Sailing yacht, classic experience under wind power, up to 10 guests, from €800 half-day.

For a modern and versatile option, consider the Olympic 490 for hire, a 4.9-metre motorboat that seats five and is perfect for exploring the shoreline near Port Grimaud or Pampelonne Bay. You can find it via the Olympic 490 listing on BoatHire24. One option worth a look is the Olympic 490 for hire.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Saint Tropez?

Prices vary by boat type and season, with shared tours from €100 per person and private motorboats from €400 half-day.

  • Shared tour (2, 3 hours): €100, €150 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €400, €700.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,200, €2,500.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (8 hours): €3,000, €6,000.

To get an accurate picture, use the boat search on BoatHire24 to filter by date and boat type; it shows live prices and availability from local operators. If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Saint Tropez?

For smaller motorboats under 20 hp, no licence is required in France, but the operator confirms exact requirements for each boat. To see how booking works, visit the boat search.

During the high season (July and August), the Mistral wind can pick up suddenly in the afternoons. Check the forecast daily and plan to return by 3pm if you are exploring beyond the bay.

In 2026, many rental boats in Saint Tropez include a VHF radio and basic safety kit. Always confirm life jacket sizes and the fuel policy with the operator before departure. BoatHire24 verifies listings, so you can book with confidence.

At a glance

  • Price band: €100, €6,000 (shared tour to crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Trip length: 2, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 1, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Saint Tropez

Can I rent a boat for a sunset cruise in Saint Tropez?

Yes, many operators offer 2-hour sunset trips leaving Port de Saint Tropez around 6pm, with prices from €150 per person.

Is it possible to anchor off Pampelonne Beach?

Yes, but in high season the anchorage gets very busy; arrive early (before 10am) to find a spot, and note the beach clubs do not allow overnight anchoring.

What should I bring for a day on the water near Saint Tropez?

Sun cream, hat, towel, swimwear, a light jacket for wind, water shoes, and snacks; most rentals supply a cooler box.

Can I hire a skipper for a boat I rent?

Yes, many listings on BoatHire24 include a skipper option for an extra €200, €400 per day, essential if you lack a licence or want local knowledge.

If you own a boat in Saint Tropez, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of renters planning their 2026 holidays; you can rent out your boat easily through their platform. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Saint Tropez or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Saint Tropez and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
